---
title: "Agents"
subtitle: "Who does what, when they run, and what you should ask them for"
---

# How Agents Fit Into Daily Use

Dittonomics uses agents to keep specialist work narrow.

The important rule is:

- creators create
- critics critique
- the main Codex session orchestrates

In normal use, you call a skill such as `$clo-discover` or `$clo-write`.
The skill then decides which agent or worker-critic pair should run.

# The Main Agent Families

## Discovery

| Agent | Role | Typical output | When it runs |
|---|---|---|---|
| `librarian` | finds and organizes literature | literature map, annotated bibliography | `$clo-discover lit` |
| `librarian_critic` | checks literature quality | coverage and recency critique | after librarian |
| `explorer` | finds and assesses data | data source list, feasibility memo | `$clo-discover data` |
| `explorer_critic` | checks data fit | measurement and validity critique | after explorer |

## Strategy

| Agent | Role | Typical output | When it runs |
|---|---|---|---|
| `strategist` | proposes identification designs | strategy memo | `$clo-strategize` |
| `strategist_critic` | pressure-tests the design | strategy review report | after strategist |

## Execution

| Agent | Role | Typical output | When it runs |
|---|---|---|---|
| `data_engineer` | cleans, wrangles, prepares figures | cleaned data products, figure inputs | `$clo-analyze` |
| `coder` | implements analysis scripts | estimation pipeline, outputs | `$clo-analyze` |
| `coder_critic` | checks execution quality | code review, strategy alignment | after coder or data_engineer |

## Writing

| Agent | Role | Typical output | When it runs |
|---|---|---|---|
| `writer` | drafts or revises sections | outline, paragraph plan, draft | `$clo-write` |
| `writer_critic` | critiques writing quality | writing review findings | proofread or writing review |
| `econ-intro-writing` | governs front-end structure | intro architecture guidance | intro, context, literature, opening stack |

## Review And Submission

| Agent | Role | Typical output | When it runs |
|---|---|---|---|
| `editor` | calibrates journal posture | editorial synthesis | peer review mode |
| `domain_referee` | checks contribution and literature positioning | domain report | peer review mode |
| `methods_referee` | checks design and inference | methods report | peer review mode |
| `verifier` | checks mechanics and package readiness | verification pass or fail | submission or compile checks |

## Talks

| Agent | Role | Typical output | When it runs |
|---|---|---|---|
| `storyteller` | builds presentations | talk deck | `$clo-talk` |
| `storyteller_critic` | checks talk quality | talk review report | after storyteller |

# What You Should Usually Call Instead

## Instead of calling agents directly, call skills

| If you want | Use |
|---|---|
| literature work | `$clo-discover lit` |
| data search | `$clo-discover data` |
| research design | `$clo-strategize` |
| coding or output pipeline | `$clo-analyze` |
| writing | `$clo-write` |
| review | `$clo-review` |
| utilities and state management | `$clo-research-tools` |

# When To Mention An Agent Anyway

Direct mention is useful when you want to shape the posture.

Examples:

```text
Use $clo-review, but make the methods_referee especially strict on the DiD assumptions.
```

```text
Use $clo-write intro and keep econ-intro-writing firmly in charge of structure.
```

```text
Use $clo-discover lit and make the librarian repo-bibliography-first before broad search.
```

# Worker-Critic Logic

The system is intentionally not one-agent-does-everything.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
  A[Worker creates artifact] --> B[Critic reviews artifact]
  B -->|Pass| C[Artifact moves forward]
  B -->|Needs work| D[Worker revises]
  D --> B
```

Why this matters:

- it exposes weak logic before it gets expensive
- it prevents self-grading
- it keeps quality review operational

# Agent Cheat Sheet

| Agent | Ask for this | Do not ask for this |
|---|---|---|
| `librarian` | literature mapping | final identification strategy |
| `explorer` | data source search | final causal claims |
| `strategist` | estimand and design | code implementation |
| `coder` | scripts and outputs | literature review |
| `writer` | manuscript sections | referee scoring |
| `writer_critic` | writing review | drafting from scratch |
| `domain_referee` | field-facing peer review | code fixes |
| `methods_referee` | methods peer review | contribution rewriting |
| `verifier` | mechanics and replication checks | substantive redesign |

# The Colleague Rule

If a colleague is new to the system, teach them this sentence first:

> Call the workflow skill you need. Let Codex decide whether an agent should be dispatched.
